page: Microsoft Azure D8d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz and is equipped with a 35 MB L3 cache. The system is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Additionally, the instance includes 307 GB of bundled local storage, though it does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results place the D8d_v4 consistently in the average performance tier for most workloads. Single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests yield scores of 1421.78 and 7443.02 respectively, while Geekbench scores reach 1237.0 for single-core and 4751.0 for multi-core operations. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 414.44 GB/sec, dropping to 46.63 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, which scores 27.25 GB/sec, placing it in the bottom 25% tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D8d_v4 offers a balanced resource density, combining dedicated compute, moderate memory, and fast local storage without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. This makes it highly suitable for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, database operations, and Redis caching layers, where it handles up to 7.06 million SET operations per second. It is also capable of running lightweight machine learning tasks, such as small-scale LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D8d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 307 GB of bundled local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers balanced performance across standard benchmarks, including a PassMark CPU score of 8537.82 and a Redis SET throughput of 7.06 million operations per second. While its memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression performance sit comfortably in the average tier, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression represents a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25%. This server is qualitatively cost-effective for balanced workloads, making it well-suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8d_v4 is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, delivering balanced performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8d_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, integrated local storage, and reliable mid-tier performance.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory paired with 307 GB of bundled local storage
Average-tier performance in database operations and static web serving
Tradeoff in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, performing in the bottom 25%
Best suited for general-purpose applications, web servers, and database hosting
